moment_text: I've always been really conscious about. Taking care of my health and preserving not only my physical health, but also my mental health. So I think for me, along that journey, I've always leaned into self-care practices without really being conscious of what they were. And I think back then, we didn't have the language. I don't know about you, but self-care now seems to be a buzzword that everyone is throwing around. But I would say a decade ago, that's not what people perceived as self-care. It wasn't mainstream. I would say, you know, along my career journey and as things started to unfold, I had a moment where I, I like to call it a little pause moment where I was at a crossroads and I had to reflect upon what exactly I wanted to do. With my life and I went to yoga school. I spent a bit of time there, and while I was at yoga school, I obviously did all of the work, or mostly the internal work I would say. And in that process I realized this is knowledge that I can actually bring to people because it's an understanding of that empirical evidence. By observation and also my own experiences and a compilation of understanding holistic practices that I learned within yoga school and also the theoretical knowledge. And that's when I had the idea of trying to combine all of this and putting it into a platform or vehicle that sort of democratizes holistic wellbeing because I don't know about you, but it's not really something that we're taught at school, and that's. Part of my missions is just analyzing systems and structures and us maybe understanding where exactly are we actually supposed to get this knowledge, because if not from our parents, we would expect to get it from the school system. But to me there seems to be an absence of this knowledge, yet it is so important and you and I both know, mental health is very critical, yet it doesn't seem to be, um, given the attention that it needs to in the manner that it needs to be.
eligibility_rationale: Passes A2 evidence gate: The moment contains clear barrier_signals (lack_of_language, education_gap) and agency_signals (making_decisions) related to a system_interaction (education_system).

moment_text: So when I went to yoga school now, I was not practicing law at that time, so it was a real moment of disconnecting from the whole world, disconnecting from that part of practice that I'd been doing for about a decade. Getting to know myself better outside of the law because as I say, I had spent over a decade, obviously, from law school going straight into private practice. I didn't necessarily know who I was outside of that, so I had lots of questions around identity to ask myself, and I do think that yoga school found me at the right time because it's probably the pivotal moment where I needed to ask myself those questions, and I don't think that I would've necessarily. moment_text: It's something that I realized is probably going to resonate with a lot of people, because I think a lot of us, we identify ourselves based on what we do, but I think when I was disconnected from what I did for the first time, because I mean, even at your infancy, you go to school quite early on, you identify as a student and then as a law student, there's no real. Space to, to self-reflect and also self-identify, which I think is so important. And I would love to loop that into mental health because I do think, uh, the rise in mental health issues is because people are not aligned with purpose. They're aligned with defined purposes and predefined boxes that society's placed, but it's not necessarily aligned with that internal purpose.

moment_text: The boundaries perspective. So I'm a recovering people pleaser. And you know, it's interesting because it's actually in my research and in my work that I truly came to understand, you know, dis-ease is your body not being at ease. And when you're not in tune with your body, your mind, and what it's saying to you. You are never gonna know what you actually truly need. And boundaries is a huge part of that because if you're not able to put a boundary and take your annual leave or put a boundary and not help somebody else who needs help because you're not feeling good and you're not too concerned about maybe being a bad sister or a bad daughter because you need a moment. I think that that's quite critical, that that's something that every woman needs to learn. It does become very difficult and when it's things that were not taught, I, you know, a lot of women have told me that it comes with age, it comes with maturing, which I think is probably true to your, to, as you say, like lived experience I think does play a role in it. But I do think that young girls are not really taught how to maintain boundaries because they are primed into being people pleasers and serving and pouring from empty cups. I do think that it comes a lot later in life that you're able to create those healthy boundaries and know when to pour and when not to pour, and when to take the moment to restore and when to give.

Oh, the one that I haven't mentioned is the props. Hopefully hoping to add on yoga mats, blogs, straps in the near future. Um, and yeah, we are just hoping that the studio is this online resource that people can just jump on at any time and take what they need.
eligibility_rationale: Passes A2 evidence gate: Contains emotional (anxiety), agency (sharing_story), and barrier (stigma) signals related to mental health tools and normalizing life changes.

moment_text: First of all, I just wanna hug them all. And say it's gonna be okay. No matter where they find themselves in the journey, it's gonna be okay. Because I think a lot of women feel alone in this journey because they're not really understood, not understood at home, not understood in the academic systems. At a corporate level, it's very isolating. But for anybody who's listening and maybe feeling that way, just know the journey. It's, it's normal. It's normal to feel that way. And as you elevate, you do isolate. Unfortunately, you shed, and that's normal.
eligibility_rationale: Passes A2 evidence gate: Contains strong emotional (isolation, validation) and barrier (social_isolation, lack_of_validation) signals related to systemic misunderstanding.
notes_for_reviewer: The speaker mentions multiple system interactions (family, education_system, workplace) which are summarized under the broader 'community' tag.

moment_text: I think that life, it is what you make of it, but it, it's also cycles. It can't always be good. It can't always be bad. But I think that it's very important that you don't self abandon and you're there for yourself at all times, because if you're not there for you, no one else is gonna be there for you. So I'm a huge advocate of self-love as well. I would also encourage women to definitely carve out moments daily to take care of themselves. Rest and restoration are so important for us as women. We are cyclical creatures. We need more rest. It's not me being anti-feminist. It is the science we do need rest. There's absolutely nothing wrong if you need to take a break. It's good for your mental health, for your physical health and for your emotional health.
eligibility_rationale: Passes A2 evidence gate: Contains strong agency signals (self_advocacy, setting_boundaries) related to self-care and preservation.

moment_text: And lastly, also just there's nothing wrong with emotions. I think the those statement that I've hated. Over these years is that women are so emotional. There's absolutely nothing wrong with emotions. It's just knowing how to manage those emotions. Um. A study show that 80% of autoimmune diseases affect women, and that's due to repressed anger. So I would encourage women to learn how to channel these negative emotions in whatever way suits them. And that's why it's important to get to know you. Forget about what the apps tell you, the studies tell you, and even maybe what we've shared in this podcast, you have a moment to yourself and you sit and you, you self-study. What is it that makes. You feel good and that's what you are going to do for you. It's not a space, it's not a time to judge yourself. I think we get enough judgment from society so you don't add yourself to the judgment list, develop whatever practice it is that works for you, and you give yourself that time because at the end of the day, you are the biggest project that you'll work on, and you are the only perpetual project that you'll work on.
eligibility_rationale: Passes A2 evidence gate: Contains emotional (anger), agency (self_advocacy), and barrier (stigma) signals related to societal judgment of women's emotions.

moment_text: So again. Hugs to all the women out there who are doing absolutely amazing things. I mean, ever since I've transitioned into this space, I've realized women are amazing community builders like yourself, Elizabeth. I mean, it could only be a woman that would decide we're gonna go under the the hood and we're going make sure that it's an intergenerational platform because you want to leave. Your legacy, a resource that women can tap into no matter what age they are. Amazing. So needed because we, some women don't have the stories. They don't have moms and sisters and aunts that they can ask about what happened to you at this age? I'm experiencing this. What about you? Amazing that we have a platform where we can tap into a resource and listen to lived experience because it's so important.
eligibility_rationale: Passes A2 evidence gate: Contains emotional (validation), agency (sharing_story), and barrier (lack_of_information) signals related to the power of community to fill knowledge gaps.

moment_text: We have to start to understand our own bodies, our own minds. The science is great. But it is a snapshot in time. Our body has our own internal engineering. We just need to connect with it. It knows exactly what to do. If you cut yourself, now your body knows it, I'm gonna bleed blood. But after that it heals. It knows how to restore itself. So we do have this internal intelligence that each of us can tap into, and that's a message for men and women. But I think I've said enough to the women out there, I think I'll leave it there.
eligibility_rationale: Passes A2 evidence gate: Contains emotional (trust, confidence) and agency (self_advocacy) signals related to developing body literacy.
